summary: Verification attempt 2 inspected the required Spexus task context, dependency reports, source-level result-layout fix, post-fix capture script/results, and all listed MZ-505 desktop/laptop screenshots. The focused result-layout fix resolved the attempt-1 settlement/final laptop blockers: deal-settlement and final-result now use a dedicated result layout with no inactive hand rows, readable score/result panels, no non-decorative clipped text, and result panel bottoms at 679.25px and 643.84px in the 1024x768 viewport, above the status band. However, the visual gate cannot pass because the contract-order action stack is clipped at both required desktop sizes: output/playwright/mz-505-contract-desktop.png and output/playwright/mz-505-contract-laptop.png show the final order action extending beneath/off the bottom status frame. Capture metrics confirm actionAreaRect.bottom=841.42 and maxVisibleButtonBottom=795.03 for 1280x800, and actionAreaRect.bottom=824.16 and maxVisibleButtonBottom=779.3 for 1024x768. That violates the required action visibility/no clipped action area contract for a normal desktop/laptop play state. Evidence remains synthetic/static visual UI evidence, not backend-live evidence.

tests_run.evidence: Read current Spexus tenant/project/component scope before reading task artifact.

Confirmed task [MZ-505] US-845 visual regression for desktop table, epic PERSONAL-EP-180, refs PERSONAL-US-845 / PERSONAL-REQ-1762 / PERSONAL-AC-2428 / PERSONAL-AC-2429, and visual-regression verification scope.

Validated JSON for MZ-501 attempt-1, MZ-502 attempt-1, MZ-503 attempt-1, MZ-504 attempt-1, MZ-505 attempt-1, MZ-505 fix-1, MZ-505 orchestrator-visual-rerun, and mz505-capture-results.json. The QA design markdown was present and readable.

tests_run.command: source/read inspection of src/ui/render.ts, src/styles.css, output/playwright/mz505-capture.mjs, fix-1.json, and orchestrator-visual-rerun.json
tests_run.evidence: Confirmed the fix introduced result-like phase handling for deal-settlement/next-deal/finished, applies card-table result-table-layout/result-zone, omits hand rows for result-like phases, and tightens result panel/table CSS. Also confirmed capture fixtures are synthetic/static UI evidence with no backend service involved.

tests_run.command: direct visual inspection of output/playwright/mz-505-*.png and metrics in output/playwright/mz505-capture-results.json
tests_run.evidence: Inspected bidding, contract, whist, play, settlement, and final screenshots at 1280x800 and 1024x768. Bidding, whist, play, settlement, and final were coherent. Settlement/final laptop blockers from attempt 1 were fixed. Contract-order screenshots still clip/obscure the bottom order action under the status area at both desktop and laptop sizes.

Did not rerun because orchestrator-visual-rerun.json records post-fix targeted tests, focused regression, npm run verify, and node output/playwright/mz505-capture.mjs all passed after the fix. The release gate still fails on manual visual inspection of regenerated artifacts.

risks.risk: Contract-order legal action area is not fully visible at required desktop/laptop sizes.
risks.evidence: output/playwright/mz-505-contract-desktop.png; output/playwright/mz-505-contract-laptop.png; output/playwright/mz505-capture-results.json reports actionAreaRect.bottom=841.42 / maxVisibleButtonBottom=795.03 for 1280x800 and actionAreaRect.bottom=824.16 / maxVisibleButtonBottom=779.3 for 1024x768.
risks.impact: A player ordering a contract cannot see all available order actions cleanly in the normal visible table frame, so AC-2428 action visibility and desktop/laptop playability are not release-ready.

evidence_matrix.evidence: All six required state families were captured at desktop and laptop sizes. Phase, actor, score, table, trick/result, and context framing are visible for bidding, whist, play, settlement, and final. Contract-order state fails because the full legal action stack is not contained in the visible desktop/laptop frame.

State/context text is readable and no non-decorative clipped text was reported, but action visibility is incomplete in contract-order screenshots. The final order action extends below/under the status frame in both output/playwright/mz-505-contract-desktop.png and output/playwright/mz-505-contract-laptop.png.

Post-fix settlement/final result screenshots are readable at laptop and desktop sizes. Result panels fit above the status line, score-change/final ranking context is readable, score panel remains visible, and inactive hand rows are absent in result-like phases.

All required screenshots plus output/playwright/mz505-capture-results.json and output/playwright/mz505-capture.mjs are present. PNG dimensions match 1280x800 desktop and 1024x768 laptop. Evidence is synthetic/static UI evidence.

No horizontal overflow and no non-decorative clipped text were reported. Result/final overlap issues from attempt 1 are fixed. Contract-order action area extends below the visible frame/status band, which is a release-blocking clipping/action visibility issue.
Post-fix orchestrator evidence records npm run verify and regenerated visual capture passing, but manual visual verification found a product layout gap in contract-order action visibility.

quality_gate_matrix.evidence: Desktop screenshots exist and were inspected for bidding, contract, whist, play, settlement, and final.
Laptop screenshots exist and were inspected for bidding, contract, whist, play, settlement, and final.
Contract-order action stack clips/extends below the visible status frame at 1280x800 and 1024x768. Result/final laptop clipping from attempt 1 is fixed.
Bidding, whist, play, and settlement action/read-only areas are visible. Contract-order legal order actions are not fully visible in the normal desktop/laptop viewport.
Card table, current trick/play area, and score rail remain readable and framed in inspected screenshots; result-like phases correctly omit inactive hand rows.
Settlement and final result panels are readable at desktop and laptop sizes, with laptop result panel bottoms above the status line and no clipped score-change/final ranking text.
npm run verify passed in orchestrator post-fix evidence, but visual release readiness fails due to contract-order action clipping.
MZ-504 attempt-1 report is present, valid JSON, status passed, and records targeted accessibility plus npm run verify passing.
Scope is synthetic/static desktop UI visual evidence with no backend-live evidence and no security-relevant behavior changes under this verifier task.
